title: Medical Assistant

rationales.taskAutomation: Rooming documentation, vitals entry, scheduling and basic charting are substantially automatable; hands-on patient prep and clinical support tasks are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI scribing and intake-automation tools aimed at ambulatory care are credible and spreading through EHR vendors.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is common in larger clinics and health-system-owned practices, spreading into independent offices.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own intake documentation, scheduling and routine follow-up messaging, but vitals-taking and direct patient prep stay physical.

tasksAtRisk: Basic chart updates and intake forms
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 31-9092 (Medical Assistants), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI intake and scheduling tools are absorbing a real share of front-desk and documentation work in clinics. Medical assistants who move toward hands-on clinical support and cross-train into higher-scope roles have the clearest path forward.
